Add API format translation for Anthropic and Google Gemini

Clients send OpenAI-format requests for any model. The proxy automatically
translates to each backend's native API format and translates responses back:

- Anthropic: path → /v1/messages, system message extraction, x-api-key header,
  content blocks → choices, input_tokens → prompt_tokens, streaming SSE events
- Gemini: path → /v1/models/{model}:generateContent, messages → contents/parts,
  assistant → model role, generationConfig with

fix(chat): restore missing 'let el=null' declaration in stream handler

Symptom:
- 对话气泡刷不出来
- console: [DEBUG-DELTA] appendMsg THREW: ReferenceError: el is not defined

Root cause:
某次 Phase 4.x 重构里把 `let el=null;` 删掉了，但注释还留着
(// NOTE: `el` 声明在 try 外，catch/recover 块也要能访问到)。
SSE 分支里 `el=appendMsg(...)` 直接对未声明变量赋值 → ReferenceError，
导致流式 delta 写不进 DOM，后端落盘但前端看不到。

(另一个 ff 分支 line 1363 有自己的 `let el=null`，这俩是不同代码路径。)

Fix:
注释后面加回 `let el=null;`。

Keep dependency installs reproducible

The project needs agents to install missing tools without handoff while preventing silent version drift afterward. This records the rule that any newly installed CLI, SDK, plugin, or Python/npm dependency must be pinned to an exact version, written into tracked project files, and kept off automatic update paths unless the user explicitly asks for an upgrade.

Wave 10.1 hotfix: bonjour на VPS ломал Gateway

Реальный кейс из чата (репорт Анатолия): клиент ставил установщик
на VPS, бот молчал, gateway циклически рестартился каждые ~45 сек
с ошибкой CIAO PROBING CANCELLED. Причина — bonjour-плагин
OpenClaw core пытается анонсировать Gateway через mDNS в локальной
сети, на VPS бесполезен и падает.

Bonjour — это плагин OpenClaw core (не наш репо). Но у нас есть
флаг --vps и мы знаем «клиент на VPS». Defensive fix во всех слоях:

fix(chat): restore inner try's closing brace - my previous fix removed one too many

上次修复时删 }}catch{}}} 的同时，把另一处的 }}catch 也改成了 }catch，
导致 inner try (JSON.parse) 没有闭合 → SyntaxError: Unexpected token 'catch' at line 1500.

正确结构：
- inner try { ... }       <- 需要 }}catch 这两个 } 中的第一个
- if(delta) { ... }       <- 需要这两个 } 中的第二个
- }catch(e){...}          <- inner try 的 catch

node --check 验证通过。

feat(word): tier-3 dotted Set — navigate existing nested OOXML

keys with 2+ levels (e.g. pBdr.top.color=FF0000) navigate into

element; missing structure returns false so curated coverage
takes over (creating nested OOXML from scratch is intentionally
out of scope here — schema-order and container disambiguation

fix(chat): SSE 流式 fetch 不能用 keepalive:true，会导致 Chrome 提前关掉 response.body

症状：新建对话第一条消息发送后前端不显示回复（typing 转半天或空气泡），切走再切回来才看到内容。

根因：fetch keepalive 模式下 streamed response body 在 Chrome 上有限制，reader 提前 close → SSE delta 读不到 → el.textContent 不更新。但服务端 /api/copilot/stream 客户端断也继续读完上游 + 落盘，所以磁盘里有内容，切回来 hydrate 就显示了。

fix: _useKeepalive=false（直接关掉，反正后端有客户端断了继续读完的兜底）

feat(word): generic dotted-attr fallback for /styles add/set

Closes most of the P0-2 long tail in one helper. The new
TypedAttributeFallback.TrySet handles dotted keys of the shape
"element.attr=value" by round-tripping through the OpenXML SDK's
InnerXml parser: if the SDK recognizes the (element, attr) pair as
typed (no extension attrs), it's a valid OOXML write; otherwise
the helper rejects and the caller falls through to the existing
single-val fallback or surfaces UNSUPPORTED.

feat(closure): precondition pre-flight before checks run

Adds _classify_precondition() + _run_precondition_preflight() helpers
in director.py. When a Deliverable has preconditions, closure
mechanically pre-flights them before running the LLM-generated check
plan:

- command-shaped (single token, no slashes) → shutil.which
- path-shaped (contains slash, or starts with ./../) → Path.exists
- opaque (port numbers, env-var requirements) → skipped

fix(upgrade): set pipefail / ErrorActionPreference so download failures abort

Caught by Codex on PR #13 review.

\`bash -c \"curl X | bash\"\` returns the inner bash's exit code, so a
\`curl\` failure (4xx/5xx, transient connectivity, DNS hiccup) produced
an empty stdin → no-op bash → exit 0. The Rust caller saw success and
\`inderes upgrade\` cheerfully announced \"Done\" while leaving the
user on the old binary. Same shape on Windows: PowerShell's default
\`\$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue

fix(chat): root-cause stuck streaming display

Two issues compounding to make assistant replies invisible:

1. Frontend race: init-time pollOne and user's send() both touched
   currentId chat. pollOne's renderChat() rebuilt the DOM, detaching
   send()'s 'el' reference. Subsequent SSE deltas wrote to ghost nodes
   (invisible). Fix: pollOne yields entire iterations when streamingChats
   has the id.

2. Backend never finalized on upstream idle/error/timeout, leaving
   _streaming:true forever.

- Build succeeds without API key

fix(aosp): document and pre-empt Ubuntu 24.04 AppArmor userns block

The AOSP build fails ~53% in with "FAILED: trusty_security_vm_*.elf" and an nsjail "mount(...) Permission denied" error. Ubuntu 24.04 restricts unprivileged user namespaces via AppArmor by default; AOSP's Soong uses nsjail to sandbox several build steps (Trusty TEE VMs, etc.) and nsjail can't set up its sandbox without those namespaces.

fix(word): set never auto-creates styles, must add first

Set on /styles/X used to silently create the style when missing,
forcing an arbitrary type=paragraph default. This violated the
project-wide Set contract — every other path (/body/p[N], /chart[N],
/section[N], /header[N]) errors when the target is missing. The
auto-create also made --prop type= ambiguous: Add owns type, Set
shouldn't infer it.

Now Set throws with the exact Add command to copy. type stays where
it belongs (Add).

feat(word): detect style integrity issues in view issues

Schema treats w:styleId as plain string (not xs:ID), so duplicate ids,
duplicate names, dangling basedOn/next/link, and basedOn cycles all
slip past validate. Surface them as structure issues with severity
matching impact: Error for duplicates and cycles (Word silently keeps
first match / breaks rendering), Warning for dangling refs (Word
falls back to Normal).

fix(a2a): skip socket.getfqdn during sidecar bind to unstall macOS

http.server.HTTPServer.server_bind() calls socket.getfqdn(host) between
bind() and listen(). On macOS — notably the GitHub Actions runners — that
reverse-DNS lookup can stall for tens of seconds, leaving the socket bound
but not listening. Callers see ECONNREFUSED until getfqdn returns, so the
e2e test harnesses (which poll port readiness) timed out even after the
deadline was bumped to 30 s.

feat(kernel): per-agent + global lane caps for trigger dispatch (#3210)

* feat(kernel): per-agent + global lane caps for trigger dispatch

Trigger dispatch (TaskPosted, MessageReceived, etc.) previously
spawned an unbounded `tokio::spawn` per match and serialized
inside `send_message_full` at the per-agent `agent_msg_locks`
mutex — effectively concurrency = 1 per agent regardless of
`session_mode`, since the dispatcher never materialized a fresh
`SessionId` to bypass the lock.

fix(vault): block reserved name "llm" on credential POST (greptile P1)

Without this guard, an operator could POST {name:"llm",data:{...}} →
the row lands at vault.secrets key_name="zombie:llm". Subsequent
DELETE /v1/workspaces/{ws}/credentials/llm routes to the BYOK handler
(which deletes key_name="llm", not "zombie:llm"), and the generic
matchWorkspaceCredential returns null for the "llm" suffix so the
generic DELETE never fires either. Net result: the row is un-deleteable
through any HTTP rou

   (内存/硬盘/存储/主存/SSD/屏幕/显示器) before encoding into search URL.
   Now the JD search keyword is clean: "Mac Studio M3 Ultra 256G 1T".

fix(dsv4): auto-disable --continuous-batching for deepseek_v4 family

The mlx_lm BatchGenerator's continuous-batching path is incompatible with
DSV4's custom 4D mHC layout. Inside DeepseekV4Model.__call__, the embedded
input is tiled to (B, T, hc_mult, hidden) — a 4D shape that BatchGenerator's
prefill + sampling pipeline does not respect (it expects plain (B, T, hidden)
for cache state and logit slicing). The result is numerically corrupt logits
producing gibberish output across all four user-f
Update README.md

Decouple the monolithic sync-package.yml into three event-driven
workflows for clearer separation of concerns and independent failure
handling:

- sync-package.yml: detect new version, push sync branch, open PR
- auto-merge.yml: triggered via workflow_run, polls mergeable, squash merges
- release.yml: triggered on push to main, matches sync commit, creates tag + release

Uses workflow_run instead of pull_request to avoid the GITHUB_TOKEN

Add remote sqlmap execution via SSH

When remote_host is configured in settings, each scan phase:
- connects to the remote host via paramiko SSH
- uploads request.txt via SFTP to /tmp/openclaw_<task_id>/
- runs sqlmap on the remote host, streams output back
- cleans up the remote temp dir on completion

kill_current() sends pkill via SSH in remote mode.
Leaving remote_host empty keeps the original local execution path.

fix(all): auto-fallback to ~/.openclaw/state/<plugin> when ~/.claw-mem isn't writable (#33)

Operator report from a Docker-style multi-user host: the gateway runs as
uid !=0 but ~/.claw-mem already exists owned by another uid. soul's
fail-fast EACCES at activate() blocks plugin load — the operator has to
set CLAW_MEM_DATA_DIR or chown before the gateway can come up.

This patch adds one extra resolver candidate to all three plugins so
the typical OpenClaw sandbox ($HOME/.openclaw/state/<plugin>)

`/slide[N]/shape[K]/p[P]/r[R] --prop kern=abc` was silently
accepted: the SetAttribute call wrote `<a:rPr ... kern="abc"/>`
even though kern is an int-typed attribute per OOXML schema. The
saved file then fails strict OpenXmlValidator with "The string
'abc' is not a valid 'Int32' value", and PowerPoint may render
unexpectedly.

Base docker-compose.yml declares openclaw-cli with
'network_mode: "service:openclaw-gateway"' so it shares the gateway's
network namespace. Compose v2 forbids combining 'network_mode' with
'networks:' on the same service:

  service openclaw-cli declares mutually exclusive network_mode and
  networks: invalid compose project

fix(install): stop orphan containers before compose_up to prevent port conflicts

On a retried install, a previous run may have left a partially-started
container still holding port 18789. compose_up then fails with:

  failed to bind host port 127.0.0.1:18789/tcp: address already in use

Run 'docker compose down --remove-orphans' before 'docker compose up'
to clean up stale containers from this compose project. Volumes are
preserved. Unrelated containers are not touched.

fix(pg-query): early-return drain when conn is not in active query state

pg.zig's `Result.drain` only early-returns when `conn._state == .idle`.
That misses a real case: `result.next()` returning null inside an
explicit transaction (BEGIN/...) consumes the final 'Z' message, and
`Conn.read` sets `_state = .transaction` (because the Z reports
transaction status 'T'), not `.idle`. Upstream drain then treats the
connection as "still in flight" and blocks on a socket read that will
never complete —

### Features

* **publisher:** Reddit vault-first multi-tenant support ([#2149](https://github.com/HankHuang0516/EClaw/issues/2149)) ([e03709d](https://github.com/HankHuang0516/EClaw/commit/e03709da809429649f47846ae47991633ff039c5))

fix(pinata): distinguish trial-exhausted from JWT scope in deploy errors

A Pinata FREE account hitting its 2hr/month runtime cap returns 403 with
body 'Free trial time exhausted'. Our generic 401/403 handler misread
that as a JWT scope problem and told users to re-paste their key — which
sent them in circles regenerating valid keys against a capped account.

Now we parse the body for trial/limit phrases and return 402 with
actionable guidance (upgrade / different account / wait for reset).

The openclaw upstream docker-compose.yml uses
${OPENCLAW_IMAGE:-openclaw:local} as the default image tag.
'openclaw:local' is not in any registry — it must be built locally from

fix(install): re-check Docker daemon liveness before compose_up

The docker_start step writes a 'done' marker into the state file, so on
subsequent runs the script skips re-starting Docker. But Docker Desktop
can be quit by the user between runs, leaving the marker stale. The next
run skips docker_start, then compose_up dies with:

  unable to get image 'openclaw:local': Cannot connect to the Docker
  daemon at unix:///Users/mo/.docker/run/docker.sock.

Idempotency requires verifying invariants,

fix(agent): rebase remote phase branch before push + show real error in log

Two bugs surfaced when sim-next phase_commit ran on the new fleet:

1. Concurrent agents can push to phase/<branch> at the same time. When
   one wins, the next push gets rejected with 'fetch first'. The agent
   would then unclaim and re-queue forever. Fix: between commit and
   push, run git pull --rebase origin <branch> (best-effort; no-op if
   the remote branch doesn't yet exist).

2. The task log truncates stderr

resolveLinkedinCreds(deviceId) reads LINKEDIN_ACCESS_TOKEN +
LINKEDIN_PERSON_URN from device vault first, falls back to env.

Phase 3: Pattern A composite tools — collapse 5–7 calls per scene to 1

Adds 7 server-internalized scene tools so the model makes a single call
per scene instead of orchestrating the 3–7 step pipeline:

- record_rest_day(reason?) — lightweight rest_day
- record_signal({signal_type,detail,severity,notification_body?,scene_name?})
  — anomaly 2.B / signal_overload (subjective symptoms)
- record_body_data({weight_kg?,body_fat_pct?,...,update_profile?,scene_name?})
  — lightweight signal_capture_cha

fix(sec_scan_compose): ignore commented-out docker.sock lines

The compose security scanner used a plain grep that flagged any
occurrence of '/var/run/docker.sock' as a dangerous mount, including
comments and documentation strings. The official openclaw repo's
docker-compose.yml has the line commented out as guidance, which caused
`./openclaw install` to abort at the compose_scan step on a fresh
clone.

Strip comments with sed first, then match only active YAML volume
entries: '(^|space)- "?/var

fix(config): write meta block to satisfy upstream suspicious-config check

v2026.4.x's gateway compares each config read against a lastKnownGood
fingerprint and flags missing-meta-vs-last-good when our regenerated
config (no meta) differs from the gateway's own previous write (had
meta). That triggers the recovery flow which spams the audit log and
attempts a .last-good promotion that EACCES'es against our hardening.

Writing our own meta block keeps hasMeta:true stable across boots so

fix(deploy): restart-agent.sh actually verifies supervise was relaunched

Old script slept 2s after pkill and declared 'not running yet — may
take a few seconds' even on success. With launchd/systemd typically
relaunching in 5-8s, every successful run reported a misleading
warning, and the operator (correctly) concluded the script wasn't
working.

New behavior:
- Capture old supervise PID before pkill
- Poll up to ~30s for a *new* supervise PID that differs
- Exit non-zero with platform-specific

run_cancel uses the .signed marker to route cancel-after-sign
(rescinded_after_sign) vs cancel-before-sign (canceled). The marker
file was never wiped between mints, so a fresh negotiation that
shared the same output_dir as a prior signed test inherited the
old marker and was routed as rescinded-after-sign on cancel.

v0.5.3: fix search_url + add login-quirk usage note

Two issues addressed:

1. search_url keyword was generated from maishou's item title, which
   contains noise ('AI电脑', '台式机', 'Z1CE001AH'). Combined with
   CJK + punctuation, the encoded URL was over-long and search engines
   tokenized it awkwardly. Switched to using the user's original query
   for clean, accurate native search.

whole time.

rule structure word-for-word. Single-word `cancel` / `Cancel` /

chore: relax security policies for local dev

## Summary

This reorganizes the monorepo around developer and agent intent instead of the old broad `packages/` bucket.

The new top-level layout is:

- `platform/` for core runtime pieces: core, daemon, daemon-rs, predictor, native
- `surfaces/` for user-facing shells: CLI, dashboard, desktop, tray, browser extension
- `integrations/` for harness and runtime integrations: connectors, OpenCode plugin, Pi/Oh My Pi extensi

blocks replay attacks on PROTECTED NADI operations:

  - Timestamp window: msg.timestamp must be within ±300s of receiver's
    wall clock. Rejects stockpiled signatures from the past.
  - Per-source payload_hash LRU: rejects exact duplicates that the same
    source has sent before. Cap is 256 hashes per source — bounds memory
    while covering the most aggr

Three logic bugs around task lifecycle

- Bug 40 (TOCTOU on final status): worker's finally read 'running' from
  DB, decided final_status='done', then wrote it. If kill landed between
  read and write, the user-initiated 'killed' got silently overwritten
  with 'done'. Fix: write everything in one SQL statement and use
  CASE WHEN status='killed' THEN 'killed' ELSE 'done' END so the kill
  state is preserved atomically while logs/results still get saved.
- Bug 41: create_task could leave a task

feat(claw-mem): chat compaction + importance scoring + capture-time denoising (2.3.0) (#32)

claw-mem 2.1.0 added chat capture, but every message went straight to
disk and got injected verbatim on the next prompt. With even modest
chat traffic the tokenBudget filled up with chitchat ("好的", "ok",
"thanks") inside hours.

This release adds the missing compaction layer so chat memory stays
useful at scale: an importance heuristic at capture time, a background
compactor that rolls batches into a sin

fix(team): reject reserved slugs at channel-create handler (#323)

Audit finding: #294 widened canAccessChannelLocked (broker.go:4078) to
treat slug == 'system' (and 'nex', 'you', 'human', '') as universally
trusted senders for synthetic posts the broker itself emits. Without a
matching invariant on channel creation, a user could POST a /channels
create with slug='system' (or any other trusted-sender slug) and the
resulting channel would be implicitly readable + writable by every
trusted-sender

guessing why the UI looked blank on a corrupt wiki repo.

/data/.openclaw/plugin-runtime-deps/openclaw-<version>-<hash>/ at boot.
With our 750 root:openclaw lock on /data/.openclaw, the openclaw user
cannot create that dir at runtime, so every plugin fails to install
its runtime deps (EACCES) and the gateway comes up as

browser, etc.).

multicast-capable network.

fix(pinata): 8s per-call timeout so catch can run + rollback OR key

Without this, a hanging Pinata relay or upstream lets the Pages Function
consume its full 30s wall-clock. Cloudflare's edge then returns a generic
502 HTML page BEFORE our catch block runs — so the OpenRouter key created
earlier in deploy.ts leaks (no revokeManagedKey, no activity_log).

Now each pinataFetch aborts after 8s and throws PinataApiError(504), the
catch in deploy.ts runs, OR key is revoked, deployment row marked fai

chore: gitignore pnpm-lock.yaml

clawg-ui is a published library, not an application — consumers don't
use the lockfile, and the `openclaw: "file:../openclaw"` devDep makes
its `file:` entries non-portable across contributor checkouts. Without
CI to enforce `--frozen-lockfile`, tracking it is more churn than
benefit.

Ignoring it prevents a stray `pnpm install` from accidentally bundling
a 7000+ line lockfile into a future PR.

fix(pinata/wizard): surface deploy errors with status + body snippet (CAN-302)

The wizard was calling res.json() directly on the deploy response. When the
backend returned a non-JSON body (CF generic 502, upstream bot-challenge
HTML, etc.) the frontend threw "Unexpected token '<', '<!DOCTYPE'..." which
told users nothing about the real failure.

Mirror the same defensive parse already used in handleVerify:
  - Read the response body as text first
  - Try JSON.parse, fall back to raw text
  - On

fix(pinata): wire settings page delete button to delete-pinata for Pinata lobsters (CAN-302)

The "刪除部署" button always called /api/agents/:name/delete-deployment
which is the Zeabur-specific tear-down. For Pinata-hosted lobsters that
endpoint either no-ops or fails — leaving orphan Pinata agents and
OpenRouter child keys after a "delete" the user thinks succeeded.

- Settings page now routes by agentHosting:
    pinata → /api/agents/:name/delete-pinata
    else   → /api/agents/:name/delete-deplo

fix(local_llm): cfg-gate Unix-only ssrf import + install URL for Windows clippy

The ssrf check_url/SsrfPolicy import and OLLAMA_INSTALL_URL constant are
only consumed by install_ollama_via_script, which is already #[cfg(unix)].
Without matching cfg gates, Windows builds flagged them as unused-imports
and dead-code under -D warnings, breaking rust.yml clippy on Windows.

fix(runtime_lock,paths): unblock Windows CI for new integration tests

Two Windows-only test failures, both from cross-platform gaps the
original patches missed:

1) `paths::root_dir()` goes through `dirs::home_dir()`, which on Windows
   reads `SHGetKnownFolderPath` and ignores `%USERPROFILE%`. The tests'
   `set_var("HOME"|"USERPROFILE", tmp.path())` redirection silently fell
   through to the real user profile; `init_runtime_full_lifecycle`'s
   sanity `assert!(root.starts_with(tmp.path()))`

whatever local OpenClaw / Claude Code happened to put in the body —
typically an ollama-naming model, which v0.2.28's bare-model inferer

directly into a bash echo command. Any backtick + ${...} content in a
PR body — totally normal in markdown code spans like room:${hostId} —

the body (e.g. "agents see humans.") toggle quote state and leak list
items like - foo

fix(api_server): cap stop-run wait at 5s so interrupt can't hang handler

task.cancel() can't preempt the run_in_executor thread running
run_conversation(), so we rely on agent.interrupt() to wake the loop.
Without a timeout, a slow/unresponsive interrupt blocks the HTTP
response indefinitely. Wrap the await in wait_for(shield(task), 5.0)
and log a warning on timeout.

Also tidy one extra space in the module docstring's /stop entry.

fix(log-reload): correct max_level_hint test assertions (#3207)

The original test asserted `reload_log_level("error")` produced
`max_level_hint() == ERROR`, which is wrong: the baseline directives
(`librefang_kernel=warn`, `librefang_runtime=warn`) remain in the live
filter on every reload, and EnvFilter::max_level_hint reports the most
permissive level any callsite could pass — so the WARN-pinned targets
dominate ERROR. Caught by CI on PR #3200.

fix(pinata): route through Deno Deploy relay to bypass CF→Pinata block (CAN-302)

Pinata's CF zone blocks any incoming request carrying CF-Connecting-IP.
Cloudflare Workers auto-inject that header on every outbound subrequest with
no documented suppression mechanism, so CF Pages Functions cannot call
agents.pinata.cloud directly. Verified 2026-04-26 — even with CF-Connecting-IP
overridden to 0.0.0.0 the rule still fires.

Architecture:
  Browser → CanFly Worker (CF) → Deno Deploy relay (NOT CF)

fix: avoid prompt_toolkit complex tempfile bug and prefer nvim first

Setting buffer.tempfile = 'prompt.md' pushed prompt_toolkit into its
complex-tempfile path, which creates a temp dir and then calls
os.makedirs() on that same path when no subdirectory is present. That
raises EEXIST before the editor can launch.

Keep prompt_toolkit on the simple tempfile path with .md suffix, and
make the editor fallback chain explicit on both surfaces:
$VISUAL -> $EDITOR -> nvim -> vim -> vi -> nano.

fix(tui): accept Alt+G as Ctrl+G fallback in VSCode/Cursor terminals

VSCode and Cursor bind Ctrl+G to "Find Next" at the editor level, so
the keystroke never reaches the embedded terminal — Ctrl+G to open
\$EDITOR was effectively dead inside those IDEs.

Alt+G is unbound in both editors and reaches the TUI cleanly as
`\x1bg` → `key.meta && ch === 'g'` after parse-keypress. Accept it
alongside the existing isAction(key, ch, 'g') check, and document the
fallback in README + the hotkeys panel.

fields that exist on Var, not on Atom — and crashes with
`'Atom' object has no attribute 'name'`.

fix(daemon): stabilize repo-layout test suite

fix(formula): LTL parser precedence — comparisons bind tighter than `->`

`parse_repr` was a flat left-associative loop with no precedence, so
`called(A) -> count(B) >= count(C)` parsed as `(called(A) -> count(B)) >= count(C)`,
which crashes the evaluator (`Implies` reaches `_resolve_arith`).

The shipped `capability/shell.yaml` §4 rule
`G(called(exec) -> count(confirm_reconfirmed) >= count(exec))`
hits this exact shape at `guard_before` time. Existing tests only
checked the parse top-level node

(bug): fix UTC double-shift in OutlookComHelpers for StartUTC/EndUTC

Outlook's StartUTC/EndUTC COM properties return DateTime(Unspecified) carrying
an already-UTC value. The existing GetOptionalDateTimeOffset called ToUniversalTime()
on all Unspecified DateTimes, treating them as local time and shifting by the
machine UTC offset a second time. On EDT (UTC-4) this caused stored event times
to be 4 hours later than correct.

Add GetOptionalUtcDateTimeOffset which wraps Unspecified DateTime with
T

ErrorHandler: short-circuit OpenRouter free-tier 429s

OpenRouter :free model variants (e.g. meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct:free)
share a global rate bucket across every OpenRouter user. Hitting that 429
returns 'is temporarily rate-limited upstream' — and that limit doesn't
clear on the 10s/20-attempt timescale our retry loop uses, so we'd just
churn for 200s before giving up.

Detect that body signature and bail immediately: try fallback chain first,
otherwise breakLoop with a hint to drop

fix(xlsx): reject reserved R/C single-letter named ranges

Excel reserves the single letters R and C (case-insensitive) for R1C1
reference notation; using either as a defined name causes Excel to
reject the file with 0x800A03EC. R8 added the cell-reference and
identifier-character validators but missed these two reserved tokens
because they are not pure cell references. Reject up front.
chore: update payloads [2026-04-25T22:12:49Z]

fix(xlsx): validate font.size range 1..409

Excel UI caps font size at 409pt (ECMA-376 §17.4.18); values above are
silently dropped or coerced. The lower bound (>0) was already enforced
in shared ParseFontSize, but the upper bound is Excel-specific (Word/PPT
have far higher limits) and lives in the Excel-only call site so the
shared helper stays handler-agnostic.

fix(xlsx): reject cell rotation outside 0-180 + 255

OOXML §18.18.20 ST_TextRotation only allows 0..180 (degrees) plus the
sentinel 255 (vertical text stack). Previously, the lower bound was
guarded via SafeParseUint (rejects negative input, R15) but the upper
bound was open: 181..254 and anything above 255 were silently accepted
and produced files Excel refuses to open. Validate before assigning.

fix(deploy): mount AgentFS at /Volumes/accfs on macOS, symlink ~/.acc/shared (CCC-uu5)

fix(windows): resolve npm ENOENT/EINVAL on Windows Node v20+ (#344)

On Windows, `npm` is a batch file (`npm.cmd`), not a binary.
`execFileSync("npm", ...)` fails with ENOENT because Node won't
resolve via PATHEXT. Node v20+ CVE-2024-27980 mitigation additionally
requires `shell: true` for `.cmd` files (EINVAL otherwise).

fix(xlsx): cell Add reuses existing row by ref

R33-2: AddRow mutates sheetData directly without updating the row-index
cache populated by FindOrCreateRow. When a prior cell op on the same
sheet had already seeded the cache, a subsequent `add cell ref=AN` on
the freshly-added row N would cache-miss and create a duplicate
<x:row r="N"> alongside the original — producing an Excel-rejected
file. Invalidate the row-index cache after the direct insert (parallel
to ShiftRowsDown's existing invalidate)

Fixed class names for type checking

fix(xlsx): warn on overlapping dataValidation sqref

Stacking a second dataValidation onto an sqref that overlaps an existing
validation is silently ignored by Excel (first wins). Detect overlap via
RangesOverlap across each space-separated range pair and throw with a
clear message rather than persist an invisible rule.
fix(web): use anchor click for pop-out to bypass popup blocker

fix(xlsx): reject cross-workbook references in namedrange refersTo

Cross-workbook patterns like '[Other.xlsx]Sheet1!$A$1' or '[1]Sheet1!$A$1'
need an externalReferences part to resolve. Without it, Excel opens the
file but formulas referencing the name show #REF!. Reject up-front with
a clear error rather than silently persist a broken defined name.

fix(xlsx): accept caption= and tableName/column aliases on slicer Add

Slicer Add already stamped caption= correctly, but the schema
definition omitted caption/tableName/column, so the CLI stripped them
before reaching the handler and silently fell back to the field name.
Schema now lists caption and exposes tableName/column as aliases for
pivotTable/field; AddSlicer accepts the same alias vocabulary and
resolves bare pivot-table names against the host sheet.

fix(xlsx): wire chart trendline displayEquation/displayRSquared

HandleSeriesDottedProperty had no case for series{N}.displayEquation
or series{N}.displayRSquared, so the setter dropped them into the
unsupported bucket while also reporting Updated. Add explicit
shortcuts that target the series' existing trendline (equivalent to
series{N}.trendline.displayEquation), so the CLI emits <c:dispEq>
and <c:dispRSqr> on the trendline as expected.

fix(xlsx): wire CF top10 rank from value= prop

Top10 conditional formatting Add now accepts value= as an alias for
rank=/top= (matching cellIs vocabulary), and rejects rank<=0 with a
clear error instead of silently coercing to 10. Also expands the
conditionalformatting schema to recognize sqref/rule/fill so the CLI
no longer strips them as 'unsupported' before dispatch.
